title: Windows Editions, Requirements, Lifecycle, and Migration Decisions · Spiral Incident Review
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs windows editions, requirements, lifecycle, and migration decisions from user impact, asset identity, safety, baseline, symptom, recent change, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, verification, prevention, escalation, documentation, and transfer evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for Windows support and migration decision packet after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Compare current Windows edition capabilities for identity, management, encryption, virtualization, remote access, updates, hardware, and licensing.
Evaluate processor, architecture, memory, storage, firmware, UEFI, Secure Boot capability, TPM, graphics, display, internet, and account requirements.
Treat Windows 10 exam knowledge as a dated legacy-support and migration case after its general support end, with current exception lookup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Windows Editions · changed case · Review Windows Editions
steps.instruction:
review case — Unsupported Windows 10 devices and specialized apps create pressure to bypass Windows 11 eligibility without current lifecycle or recovery evidence. The review variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Compare current Windows edition capabilities for identity, management, encryption, virtualization, remote access, updates, hardware, and licensing.

Which action demonstrates Windows Editions without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Windows Editions · changed case · Review Windows Editions.

stimulus.title: Windows Migration Choice case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Unsupported Windows 10 devices and specialized apps create pressure to bypass Windows 11 eligibility without current lifecycle or recovery evidence. The review variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Choose upgrade, replace, isolate, extend, virtualize, retire, or redesign using support, hardware, application, data, risk, cost, access, and rollback evidence.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Bypassing hardware checks is equivalent to obtaining supported compatibility.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose upgrade, replace, isolate, extend, virtualize, retire, or redesign using support, hardware, application, data, risk, cost, access, and rollback evidence. Artifact under review: Windows support and migration decision packet.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Windows Migration Choice, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Windows Migration Choice.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose upgrade, replace, isolate, extend, virtualize, retire, or redesign using support, hardware, application, data, risk, cost, access, and rollback evidence.
